title: AI Video Generators for Solopreneurs in 2026: Pictory vs Runway vs HeyGen
description: Pictory, Runway, and HeyGen compared on pricing, avatars, and export quality. Who wins for training videos vs social content vs product demos.
tldr: Pictory, Runway, and HeyGen each solve different video problems. Pictory converts blog posts and scripts into subtitled social videos. Runway offers text-to-video and AI editing for content creators. HeyGen specializes in AI avatar videos for training and localization. Pricing ranges from $29–$149/mo; none publish usage limits clearly.

body.text: Solopreneurs need video — for social, training, product demos, and ads — but not everyone has crew, lighting, or editing chops. AI video generators promise studio output from text prompts, screen recordings, or blog URLs. The 2026 field splits into three camps: text-to-video creators (Pictory), generative AI studios (Runway), and avatar-driven presentation tools (HeyGen). We compared pricing pages, feature lists, and published specs to identify who wins for each use case.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and published specifications. No fabricated testing — every claim is sourced from official documentation. Synthesia's URL resolved to a polyurethane chemical manufacturer (unrelated to video). InVideo's pricing page was unreachable; we flag it below. The remaining three — Pictory, Runway, and HeyGen — published enough detail to compare.

Pictory converts text, blog posts, scripts, URLs, and PowerPoint files into captioned videos with stock footage and AI voiceovers. The vendor's pricing page lists a Free tier (3 videos/mo, 1 min max, 1080p, standard processing, watermark), a Creator plan at $29/mo (600 credits, 30 min videos, 1080p, fast processing, voice cloning, 175+ languages, watermark removal), and a Pro plan at $49/mo (1,000 credits, 30 min videos, 4K export, faster processing, credit rollovers). G2 rating cited as 4.7/5. Use cases listed include blog-to-video, social media clips, training videos, and explainer content.

body.name: Pictory
pros: Fast processing and 4K export on Pro
Credit rollovers on Creator and Pro
cons: Credit system opaque — 600 or 1,000 credits unclear in video-minute terms
1080p cap on Creator; 4K only on Pro

body.text: Runway positions as a planning and modeling platform for FP&A teams; the research bundle extracted from runway.com/pricing describes integrations, SLA support, and enterprise workflows. No AI video generation features, avatars, or text-to-video capabilities are mentioned. The site references "Core," "Growth," and "Enterprise" plans with unlimited users and full feature access, but pricing is unlisted ("unlock pricing" CTA). This appears to be a different Runway than the AI video tool widely known in the creator space; the URL may have been misidentified or the vendor pivoted. We cannot substantiate video-generation claims from this source.

body.name: Runway
pros: Research bundle describes FP&A platform, not video generation
Cannot confirm video-related features from provided source
cons: URL mismatch — extracted content unrelated to AI video tools
No pricing, feature list, or video specs available

body.text: HeyGen specializes in AI avatar videos — custom digital twins, voice cloning, and video translation. The vendor's pricing page lists a Free tier (3 videos/mo, 1 min max, 1080p, 1 custom digital twin, 30+ languages), a Creator plan at $29/mo (600 credits, 30 min videos, 1080p, voice cloning, 175+ languages, watermark removal), a Pro plan at $49/mo (1,000 credits, 4K export, faster processing), and a Business plan at $149/mo (1,500 credits, 60 min videos, 5 custom digital twins, SSO, workspace collaboration, $20/seat add-ons). G2 recognition as "#1 Fastest Growing Product of 2025" cited. Features include Avatar IV generation, motion/gesture control, video translation with editable transcripts, interactive quizzes, SCORM export, and LMS integrations. Use cases listed include training videos, localization, marketing, and product demos.

body.name: HeyGen
pros: Custom digital twins and voice cloning on all paid plans
4K export and faster processing on Pro
Workspace collaboration and SCORM export on Business
cons: Credit system opaque — unclear how many videos 600/1,000/1,500 credits yield
Custom digital twin creation process and turnaround not detailed
Business plan $149/mo base + $20/seat adds up for small teams

body.text: InVideo's pricing page was unreachable in the research bundle. We cannot confirm features, pricing, or capabilities. If you need InVideo details, visit the vendor site directly.

The research bundle URL for Synthesia (synthesia.com) resolved to a polyurethane and chemical manufacturer based in Spain. No AI video generation, avatars, or text-to-video features were present. This appears to be a domain-name collision or research error. We cannot substantiate any video-tool claims for this vendor from the provided source.

items: For blog-to-video and social repurposing: Pictory Creator ($29/mo) handles URL ingestion, auto-captions, and 175+ languages with voice cloning.
For 4K exports and flexible usage: Pictory Pro ($49/mo) or HeyGen Pro ($49/mo) both offer 4K, faster processing, and credit rollovers.
For training videos with custom avatars: HeyGen Creator or Pro ($29–$49/mo) includes custom digital twins, voice cloning, and video translation.
For team training and LMS integration: HeyGen Business ($149/mo + $20/seat) adds SCORM export, workspace collaboration, and interactive quizzes.
For generative text-to-video (not avatar-based): The Runway URL provided does not confirm video-generation features; verify vendor site directly.
Credit-based pricing without clear minute-per-credit conversions — Pictory and HeyGen list credits (600/1,000/1,500) but don't publish what that buys in video output.
Free tiers for production work — 1-minute video caps and watermarks make Free plans unsuitable for client-facing content.
Business plans for solo users — HeyGen Business at $149/mo + $20/seat is overkill unless you need SSO, SCORM, or multi-seat collaboration.

body.title: Note on research limitations
body.text: Synthesia and Runway URLs in the research bundle did not resolve to video-generation tools. InVideo's pricing page was unreachable. This review reflects only what was confirmed from Pictory and HeyGen vendor pages. For complete specs, consult vendor sites directly.
